objective: Raise the MiniMax M2.7 AutoRound INT4 4x B70 target from the earlier >30 tok/s bring-up goal to a quality-preserving 60+ tok/s optimization target.

model.primaryEngine: vLLM/XPU with local llm-scaler U4 decode kernels

targets.stretchCondition: Only count 75+ tok/s or 90+ tok/s if achieved through target-verified speculative decoding/MTP or deeper source-level fusion that preserves target logits. Record acceptance behavior and prefill/TTFT fields where the benchmark source exposes them.

currentQualityAnchors.label: quality-conservative long anchor
currentQualityAnchors.qualityNotes: Q/K TP variance allreduce enabled; no speculation; no expert dropping; no power-limit change.

currentQualityAnchors.label: short accepted speed point, later treated as scheduling clue if AOT graph lacks visible Q/K variance allreduce

qualityGuardrails: Do not skip Q/K RMS TP variance allreduce.
Do not drop experts or silently route missing experts.
Do not promote deferred reductions that cross RMSNorm, router, MoE, or attention nonlinearities without token/logit equivalence checks.
Do not count a lower-quality quantization switch as a MiniMax AutoRound speed improvement unless it is labeled separately.
Do not count speculative decoding unless the target model verifies accepted tokens.

externalReferences.title: vLLM speculative decoding documentation
externalReferences.use: Spec methods include ngram, suffix, MTP, Eagle3, and DFlash. Treat speculation as quality-preserving only through target verification.

externalReferences.title: vLLM Intel AutoRound quantization documentation
externalReferences.use: Intel-platform recipes currently include W4A16 and W8A16, matching the MiniMax AutoRound W4A16 path.

externalReferences.title: Intel llm-scaler
externalReferences.use: Advertises Arc Pro B60/B70 support, CCL P2P/USM, INT4/FP8 serving, and tensor/pipeline/data parallelism; mine for transferable kernels and scheduling ideas.

externalReferences.title: MiniMax M2.7 hardware performance report
externalReferences.use: Recent non-Intel numbers at 32k context include 71.52 tok/s on 4x RTX 4090, 118.74 tok/s on RTX PRO 6000, and 24.41 tok/s on DGX Spark; backend/quant differ but support a higher aspiration target.

externalReferences.title: DFlash paper
externalReferences.use: Technically interesting speculative path; local MiniMax B70 DFlash currently stalls before producing a benchmark result.

externalReferences.title: SGLang expert parallelism documentation
externalReferences.use: Current fast MoE serving leans on routed backends such as DeepEP/DeepGEMM and FlashInfer/TRT-LLM. The XPU lesson is to reduce generic TP collectives through routed-MoE and epilogue-aware kernels.

externalReferences.title: KTransformers benchmark board
externalReferences.use: Shows strong decode results on RTX 5090 systems and reinforces that specialized model/backbone kernels can beat generic TP layouts.

externalReferences.title: MiniMax-M2.7 REAP AutoRound W4A16 variant
externalReferences.use: A pruned-expert speed/fit variant; useful as a separate quality tradeoff track, not a replacement for the current full MiniMax target.

nextImplementationPreference: Keep active benchmarks on the clean quality path unless a run is explicitly experimental.
Avoid Python custom-op wrappers around dist.all_reduce after the p512/n512 regression.
Implement the next fusion in C++/SYCL or as a real compiler pass.
Start with MiniMax-specific TP4 decode shapes: Q/K RMS variance allreduce plus RMS apply, or hidden-state allreduce immediately followed by residual add/RMSNorm.
Archive each negative/default-off patch with logs and leave its env flag unset.
